name: Soda Data Quality API
what_it_does: Data quality testing framework and cloud platform with REST API for defining, running, and monitoring data quality checks (freshness, completeness, uniqueness, accuracy) across SQL databases, data warehouses, and data lakes.
use_cases: Automated data quality gate checks in ETL/ELT pipeline agents
Monitoring data freshness and row count expectations after ingestion jobs
Querying historical check results to detect data drift over time
Triggering data quality scans programmatically via API as part of CI/CD
Sending data quality alerts to Slack/PagerDuty when checks fail
not_for: Real-time stream quality monitoring (use dedicated stream processors)
Organizations without a dedicated data engineering team to define checks
best_when: An agent orchestrates data pipelines and needs to validate data quality at each stage before proceeding.
avoid_when: You need lightweight, developer-local data testing without cloud dependencies (use Great Expectations or dbt tests instead).

auth.notes: Soda Cloud uses API keys for programmatic access. SCIM provisioning supported for enterprise. OAuth2 for external integrations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Soda Core (open-source): fully free, unlimited checks
pricing.paid_tiers: Soda Cloud from $99/month; Enterprise custom pricing
pricing.notes: soda-core Python library is Apache 2.0 open-source. Soda Cloud adds centralized monitoring, alerting, and collaboration. Many use just the open-source library.

performance.rate_limits: No publicly documented rate limits for API calls

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Soda Core has well-structured Python exceptions. Soda Cloud REST API returns JSON error objects with descriptive messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Check definitions are declarative — same SodaCL file produces the same checks. Running the same scan twice gives consistent results (data-dependent).
known_agent_gotchas: Soda Core runs checks locally against databases; cloud API is for metadata/reporting only
SodaCL (Soda Check Language) is YAML-based and requires learning the check syntax
Database credentials must be managed separately from Soda configuration
Large scans against data warehouses can be slow and expensive (BigQuery/Snowflake costs)

security_score_components.security_notes: Credentials for databases are managed locally (not sent to Soda Cloud). Soda Core is open-source and auditable. SOC2 Type II certified for Soda Cloud.
